title: IGAD In-Country Training and Mentorship for Women and Youth MSMEs on Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade 2026: Fully Sponsored Regional Capacity Building Programme for Entrepreneurs in East Africa
summary: Applications are now open for the IGAD In-Country Training and Mentorship for Women and Youth MSMEs on Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade 2026, a regional initiative under the Horn of Africa Digital Market Integration (HDMI) Project – Phase I. Organized by the Intergovernmental Authority on Development (IGAD), this important programme seeks to strengthen the capacity of women and youth-led micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) across the IGAD region through specialized training and mentorship on digital trade, e-commerce, digital financial services, and cross-border market opportunities. The programme will run from July to December 2026 across multiple IGAD Member States and is specifically designed to empower entrepreneurs with practical digital trade skills that will help them access regional and global markets, leverage technology for business growth, and benefit from opportunities created through the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA). As Africa continues to accelerate digital transformation and regional economic integration, women and youth entrepreneurs remain central to building inclusive economic growth. However, gender inequalities, limited access to technology, digital literacy gaps, and restricted financial inclusion continue to affect the participation of women and youth in digital trade ecosystems. Through this initiative, IGAD aims to close these gaps by equipping MSMEs with the tools, knowledge, and mentorship required to compete successfully in today’s digital economy. The training programme will use the Gender Action Learning System (GALS) methodology, a participatory and inclusive approach designed to strengthen gender-sensitive economic empowerment and business development. The initiative combines practical in-person workshops with online mentorship and continuous engagement sessions. About IGAD and the HDMI Project The Intergovernmental Authority on Development (IGAD) is a regional economic community focused on promoting peace, security, economic integration, and sustainable development across the Horn of Africa and East Africa region. The Horn of Africa Digital Market Integration (HDMI) Project – Phase I is implemented under IGAD’s Economic Cooperation and Regional Integration Division (ECRID). The project operates in: Djibouti Ethiopia Somalia South Sudan The broader objective of the HDMI Project is to strengthen regional economic integration by creating a more connected and inclusive digital market across the IGAD region. The project supports: Digital trade systems E-commerce development Cybersecurity Digital transactions Data protection Cross-border digital services Innovation ecosystems Digital entrepreneurship The programme aligns with: African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) IGAD Vision 2050 IGAD Strategy 2026–2030 According to IGAD, digital transformation has the potential to: Create jobs for women and youth Expand market access Support entrepreneurship Increase competitiveness Promote innovation Strengthen regional integration Improve trade efficiency Why This Training Programme Matters Digital trade is becoming one of the fastest-growing sectors in Africa’s economy. Businesses that can effectively use: E-commerce platforms Digital payment systems Online marketing Mobile money Digital financial tools are increasingly gaining access to larger markets and greater economic opportunities. However, many women and youth entrepreneurs still face challenges such as: Limited digital literacy Lack of access to digital platforms Financial exclusion Poor access to market information Limited knowledge of cross-border trade opportunities Weak digital marketing skills The IGAD Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade Programme is designed to address these challenges directly by helping MSMEs build practical skills and confidence in digital business operations. Programme Objectives The programme aims to: Strengthen the digital trade capacity of women and youth MSMEs Promote gender-sensitive participation in digital economies Increase access to digital financial services Support participation in e-commerce Improve financial literacy and business management Expand access to regional and international markets Facilitate inclusion in AfCFTA trade opportunities Build sustainable entrepreneur networks across the region Training Structure and Format The programme will be delivered in a hybrid format consisting of both in-person and online sessions. 1. In-Person Training Workshops Participants will attend a three-day in-country workshop delivered by trained Gender Champions using the GALS methodology. The in-person sessions are designed to: Encourage active participation Facilitate practical learning Offer real-time feedback Build hands-on experience Strengthen peer learning Enable networking among entrepreneurs Participants will receive practical guidance on applying digital tools to real business situations. 2. Online Mentorship and Follow-Up Sessions After the physical training workshops, participants will continue engaging through online mentorship sessions hosted via IGAD social platforms. The online component will: Reinforce learning outcomes Provide ongoing mentorship Support continuous knowledge exchange Facilitate networking Encourage collaboration among participants Share additional digital trade resources This blended learning approach ensures long-term impact and sustained entrepreneurial growth. Training Locations The programme will take place in the following cities across the IGAD region: Djibouti – Djibouti Addis Ababa – Ethiopia Nairobi – Kenya Mogadishu – Somalia Juba – South Sudan Port Sudan – Sudan Kampala – Uganda These locations will host the in-person training workshops between July and December 2026. What Participants Will Learn The training programme covers several key components of digital entrepreneurship and online trade. 1. Digital Financial Services Participants will learn: Mobile money systems Digital payment platforms Financial inclusion tools Online transaction management Secure financial practices 2. E-Commerce and Online Business Opportunities Training topics include: Selling products online E-commerce platforms Online customer engagement Digital business models Marketplace participation 3. Digital Marketing Strategies Participants will gain skills in: Social media marketing Online branding Digital customer outreach Content marketing Audience engagement 4. Financial Literacy and Business Management The programme will help entrepreneurs improve: Financial planning Budgeting Business sustainability Revenue management Entrepreneurial decision-making 5. Safe Participation in Digital Spaces Participants will learn: Cybersecurity basics Digital safety practices Online privacy protection Secure digital communication 6. Cross-Border Trade and AfCFTA Opportunities The programme will provide guidance on: Regional trade opportunities Export readiness Cross-border e-commerce Market access within Africa AfCFTA opportunities for MSMEs Benefits of the Programme Selected participants will receive numerous benefits, including: Capacity Building Participants will develop practical digital trade skills that can directly improve business performance. Access to Mentorship The programme offers continued mentorship and support after the physical training workshops. Networking Opportunities Participants will become part of a growing regional network of entrepreneurs, trainers, and digital trade advocates. Exposure to Regional Markets Entrepreneurs will gain insights into regional and international market opportunities. Improved Financial Inclusion Participants will learn how to leverage digital financial tools and services for business growth. Business Expansion Opportunities The programme supports MSMEs in scaling their businesses through digital solutions and e-commerce strategies. Participation at No Cost Participation in the programme is completely free for selected applicants. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a woman or youth entrepreneur Actively run a micro, small, or medium enterprise (MSME) Possess a valid business license Demonstrate interest in digital trade Show commitment to expanding their business using digital platforms Commit to attending the full three-day in-person workshop Participate in online mentorship sessions IGAD notes that priority will be given to women entrepreneurs. Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for: Women entrepreneurs Youth-led startups MSME owners E-commerce business owners Small business operators Digital entrepreneurs Community business leaders Social entrepreneurs Traders seeking regional expansion Entrepreneurs interested in AfCFTA opportunities Expected Outcomes After completing the programme, participants are expected to: Gain stronger digital business skills Improve business competitiveness Increase participation in online trade Expand market reach Improve financial management Enhance digital literacy Access new regional markets Strengthen cross-border business participation The programme also seeks to contribute to broader regional goals such as: Economic integration Youth employment Women’s empowerment Digital transformation Inclusive economic development Required Application Documents Interested applicants must submit: Completed online application form Brief motivation statement explaining why they want to join the programme Copy of business license Short business description Explanation of how they intend to use the acquired skills Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the IGAD Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade Training and Mentorship Programme is: June 13, 2026 Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications early due to the expected high number of applications. How to Apply Applicants should complete the online application form through the official IGAD application portal. Official Application Link: https://igad.int/hdmicallforapplicationincountrytraining/ Important Notes Participation is free for selected applicants Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted Training logistics will be shared with successful participants Full participation is expected during the programme Visit HERE to learn more about the IGAD In-Country Training and Mentorship for Women and Youth MSMEs. Why Entrepreneurs Should Not Miss This Opportunity The digital economy is rapidly transforming the future of trade in Africa. Entrepreneurs who can successfully adopt digital solutions are increasingly better positioned to: Expand their customer base Improve operational efficiency Access financial services Participate in cross-border trade Increase revenue opportunities This IGAD programme offers a rare opportunity for women and youth MSMEs in East Africa and the Horn of Africa to receive free high-quality training, mentorship, and regional networking support that can significantly strengthen their businesses and future growth prospects. For entrepreneurs seeking to thrive in Africa’s growing digital economy and benefit from regional integration opportunities under AfCFTA, this initiative provides timely and practical support for long-term success. Discover more global Training and Mentorship opportunities on OFY. The post IGAD In-Country Training and Mentorship for Women and Youth MSMEs on Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade 2026: Fully Sponsored Regional Capacity Building Programme for Entrepreneurs in East Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy 2026 in Greece: Leadership Beyond Boundaries Programme for Emerging Mediterranean Leaders
summary: Applications are now open for the 6th ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy 2026 , an international leadership and geopolitical training programme taking place in Ancient Olympia, Greece, from July 13–17, 2026. Organized by the Hellenic Foundation for European and Foreign Policy (ELIAMEP) in collaboration with the Institute of Global Affairs at The American College of Greece (ACG) , this prestigious cross-border educational initiative brings together graduate students, young professionals, policy experts, researchers, diplomats, journalists, and future leaders from across the Mediterranean region and beyond. Under the theme “Leadership Beyond Boundaries: Empowering the Next Generation in an Age of Geopolitical Flux,” the 2026 Summer Academy aims to equip emerging leaders with advanced knowledge, strategic leadership skills, negotiation techniques, and deeper understanding of global and regional geopolitical developments affecting Europe, the Mediterranean, the Middle East, and neighboring regions. Hosted at the International Olympic Academy in Olympia, Peloponnese, Greece, the programme provides participants with an immersive academic and leadership experience in one of the world’s most symbolic locations for peace, international cooperation, dialogue, and intercultural exchange. As geopolitical tensions, economic uncertainty, energy security concerns, migration pressures, climate challenges, and shifting global alliances continue reshaping the international landscape, programmes like the ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy have become increasingly important for preparing the next generation of leaders capable of navigating complex global realities. The Academy combines academic rigor, leadership development, networking opportunities, practical workshops, and policy-oriented discussions to empower participants with the tools necessary to contribute meaningfully to international cooperation, diplomacy, strategic policymaking, and regional stability. About the Organizers Hellenic Foundation for European and Foreign Policy (ELIAMEP) ELIAMEP is one of Greece’s leading independent non-profit think tanks focused on: European affairs International relations Mediterranean studies Foreign policy Security and defense Migration Climate and sustainability Regional cooperation The organization has established itself as a major policy research institution in Europe and the Mediterranean, contributing to dialogue and analysis on some of the most pressing global challenges. Institute of Global Affairs at The American College of Greece (ACG) The Institute of Global Affairs at ACG supports research, education, and policy engagement related to: Global governance International diplomacy Leadership development Regional cooperation Public policy Geopolitical transformation Together, ELIAMEP and ACG have successfully organized multiple editions of the Summer Academy, attracting participants from Europe, North Africa, the Middle East, and other parts of the world. About the 6th ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy 2026 The 2026 edition of the Summer Academy will focus heavily on leadership development in an increasingly uncertain and rapidly evolving geopolitical environment. Participants will engage with: Academics Policymakers Diplomats Journalists Researchers Business leaders International affairs experts The Academy emphasizes interdisciplinary learning and cross-cultural dialogue while helping participants develop practical leadership and policy skills. The programme is designed to foster: Strategic thinking Leadership capacity International cooperation Regional understanding Policy analysis Negotiation competence Public speaking confidence Collaborative problem-solving Location and Dates Programme Dates July 13–17, 2026 Venue International Olympic Academy Olympia, Peloponnese, Greece The venue is located near the historic site of Ancient Olympia, globally recognized as a symbol of peace, unity, and international cooperation. The location itself offers participants a unique atmosphere for dialogue, reflection, and intercultural exchange. Why This Summer Academy Matters The Mediterranean region continues to experience major geopolitical transformations influenced by: Strategic competition between global powers Regional instability Migration challenges Climate change Energy transitions Security concerns Economic uncertainty Technological disruptions Emerging leaders and professionals working in policy, diplomacy, governance, media, and civil society increasingly require: Cross-border collaboration skills Strategic foresight Negotiation expertise International leadership capabilities The ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy addresses these needs directly through an intensive leadership and policy training programme. Key Themes of the 2026 Summer Academy 1. Global Geopolitical and Geoeconomic Shifts Participants will explore: Shifting global power dynamics Strategic competition Emerging economic alliances Regional instability New geopolitical opportunities International security trends The programme examines how these developments affect: Europe The Mediterranean The Middle East Africa International governance systems 2. The Role of Global and Regional Powers in the Mediterranean The Academy will analyze how major actors such as: European Union United States Russia China Gulf States MENA countries shape political, economic, and security developments across the Mediterranean region. Participants will gain deeper understanding of: Strategic interests Regional influence Diplomacy International cooperation Competition among powers 3. Europe’s Strategic Interests in the Region Discussions will focus on: European security Migration management Energy security Trade partnerships Regional resilience EU foreign policy Mediterranean governance frameworks Participants will evaluate how Europe seeks to balance: Stability Cooperation Economic interests Human security Climate priorities 4. Conflict Management and Policy Bridge-Building One of the programme’s major components focuses on: Diplomacy Peacebuilding Mediation Inclusive governance Regional integration Sustainable conflict resolution Participants will study: Drivers of regional conflict Policy negotiation frameworks Mechanisms for dialogue Multilateral cooperation strategies Interactive Workshops and Practical Skills Training The Academy includes highly practical leadership-focused workshops. Strategic Foresight Skills Participants will learn: Scenario planning Long-term strategic analysis Anticipating geopolitical risks Decision-making under uncertainty Advanced Negotiation Techniques Workshops will strengthen participants’ abilities in: Diplomatic negotiations Conflict mediation Consensus building Stakeholder engagement Public Speaking and Media Training Participants will receive training on: Communication strategies Public engagement Media interaction Policy communication Leadership messaging These practical sessions aim to strengthen participants’ confidence and leadership presence in professional and international environments. Programme Structure The Summer Academy combines: Academic lectures Interactive seminars Policy discussions Leadership workshops Group activities Networking opportunities Collaborative learning sessions Participants will engage directly with experts and peers from diverse cultural and professional backgrounds. Who Should Apply? The programme is ideal for: Graduate students Young professionals Emerging leaders Diplomats Researchers Journalists Civil society representatives Policy practitioners Think tank analysts The Summer Academy particularly targets individuals interested in: International relations Diplomacy Public policy Mediterranean studies European affairs Global governance Security studies Geopolitics Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Hold at least a postgraduate or Master’s degree in a relevant field Demonstrate leadership potential Be proficient in English Be 35 years old or younger All programme sessions will be conducted in English. Required Application Documents Applicants must submit the following documents in English: 1. Statement of Purpose Maximum 200 words explaining: Why the Summer Academy is relevant to their professional development Their motivation for applying Their leadership interests 2. Curriculum Vitae (CV) 3. Proof of English Proficiency Applicants are encouraged to ensure their documents clearly demonstrate: Leadership experience Academic preparation Professional aspirations Interest in international affairs Participation Fees and Deadlines Early Bird Application Cost: €350 Deadline: March 31, 2026 Regular Application Cost: €450 Deadline: June 15, 2026 What the Participation Fee Covers The organizers will cover: Accommodation Full-board meals Local transportation Conference-related activities Guided tours Archaeological site visits Participants are responsible for: International travel expenses Visa costs (if applicable) Personal expenses Benefits of Participating Participants will gain: Advanced Leadership Skills The programme focuses heavily on practical leadership development and strategic thinking. International Networking Opportunities Participants will connect with: Future leaders Policymakers Diplomats Academics Researchers Journalists Exposure to High-Level Experts Sessions will be facilitated by renowned professionals and scholars in international affairs. Enhanced Career Development The programme strengthens participants’ professional profiles in: Diplomacy Public policy International relations Research Governance Cross-Cultural Collaboration Participants will engage with peers from multiple countries and backgrounds. Mediterranean Geopolitical Expertise The Academy provides deep regional insights highly relevant to current international affairs. Why Ancient Olympia Is a Powerful Venue Hosting the Academy in Ancient Olympia carries symbolic significance because the site historically represents: Peace Dialogue International cooperation Shared human values The environment encourages participants to reflect on: Global leadership Ethical governance Cooperation across cultures How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their applications through the Official ELIAMEP Summer Academy Platform . Applicants are encouraged to apply early, particularly to benefit from the reduced early bird fee. Application Deadline Regular application deadline: June 15, 2026 Contact Information For additional inquiries: SummerAcademy@eliamep.gr Why Young Leaders Should Apply In today’s increasingly fragmented geopolitical environment, leadership requires: Strategic awareness Cross-cultural communication Policy understanding Negotiation competence Ethical decision-making The ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy offers emerging leaders a rare opportunity to: Build these essential skills Expand international networks Learn from experts Engage in meaningful dialogue Strengthen their leadership capacity For young professionals and graduate students seeking to contribute to regional cooperation, diplomacy, governance, and international policymaking, this programme offers a transformative educational and professional experience in one of the world’s most historic and inspiring locations. Visit HERE to learn more about the ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy 2026 in Greece. Discover more global Conference , Training , and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY. The post ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy 2026 in Greece: Leadership Beyond Boundaries Programme for Emerging Mediterranean Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNITAR–FAO Trade, Food Security and Nutrition (TFSN) Course 2026: Free Online Training Opportunity for Policymakers, Researchers, and Agricultural Trade Professionals in Africa
summary: The United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR), in partnership with the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), is offering a fully funded online training programme titled Trade, Food Security and Nutrition (TFSN) . This highly specialized course is designed to strengthen the capacity of policymakers, government officials, researchers, and agricultural trade professionals across Eastern and Southern Africa. Scheduled to run from 13 July 2026 to 14 August 2026 , this five-week e-learning programme provides participants with a unique opportunity to deepen their understanding of the complex relationship between agricultural trade, food security, nutrition, and sustainable development. The course is offered completely free of charge and is delivered online, making it accessible to professionals across multiple African countries without the need for travel. Successful participants will receive a certificate of completion from UNITAR and FAO. Interested applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early as places are limited and participation is subject to a competitive selection process. Why This Course Matters Food security remains one of the most pressing development challenges facing Africa. At the same time, trade is increasingly recognized as a powerful tool for promoting economic growth, agricultural transformation, and poverty reduction. The relationship between trade and food security has become a major focus of global development discussions, particularly as countries work toward achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), including the goal of ending hunger by 2030. Through the 2014 Malabo Declaration, African governments committed to increasing intra-African agricultural trade and leveraging regional and international markets to stimulate agricultural development. However, expanding trade alone does not automatically improve food security. Policymakers must carefully design trade policies that enhance agricultural productivity while ensuring access to nutritious food and supporting vulnerable populations. This course addresses these critical issues by helping participants understand how trade policies can support food security, nutrition, and sustainable development. About the Trade, Food Security and Nutrition Course The Trade, Food Security and Nutrition (TFSN) Course aims to strengthen institutional and individual capacity across Eastern and Southern Africa. Participants will learn how to: Analyze the relationship between trade and food security Understand the effects of agricultural trade on nutrition outcomes Evaluate trade policy measures Formulate evidence-based trade policies Negotiate trade agreements effectively Assess food security implications of trade liberalization Understand international trade governance frameworks Promote policy coherence between trade and nutrition objectives The course combines theoretical foundations with practical regional experiences and case studies relevant to Africa s agricultural and economic realities. Learning Objectives By the end of the programme, participants will be able to: 1. Assess Trade and Food Security Challenges Participants will gain the ability to: Identify opportunities created by trade liberalization Evaluate risks associated with increased market openness Analyze food security outcomes resulting from trade policies 2. Understand Trade Policy Measures Learners will explore: Different types of trade interventions Agricultural support policies Trade facilitation mechanisms Policy design considerations Implementation challenges 3. Examine Global Trade Governance Participants will study: The World Trade Organization (WTO) Agreement on Agriculture Global trade governance systems Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) International food security frameworks 4. Evaluate Trade and Nutrition Linkages The course will help participants understand: How trade influences food prices The impact of trade on dietary diversity Nutrient availability and food imports Policy coherence between nutrition and trade agendas Course Structure and Curriculum The programme is divided into four comprehensive units. Unit 1: Introduction to Trade and Food Security Lesson 1.1: Definitions and Linkages Between Trade and Food Security Topics include: Trade fundamentals Food security concepts Economic linkages Agricultural market dynamics Lesson 1.2: Impacts of Trade on Food Security Participants will examine: Positive and negative impacts of trade Household food access Agricultural productivity Market integration Unit 2: Trade Policy Supportive of Food Security Lesson 2.1: Trade Policy Measures and Agricultural Development Areas covered include: Tariffs Trade restrictions Agricultural support measures Market interventions Lesson 2.2: Design and Implementation of Trade Policy Measures Participants will learn: Policy formulation approaches Implementation frameworks Monitoring and evaluation mechanisms Unit 3: Governance of Trade and Food Security Lesson 3.1: Introduction to the Multilateral Trading System Topics include: WTO fundamentals Global trade agreements International trade governance Lesson 3.2: Policy Space for Food Security in the Multilateral Trading System Participants will explore: National policy flexibility Trade obligations Food security safeguards Lesson 3.3: Towards Improved Governance for Trade and Food Security Focus areas include: Institutional coordination Governance reforms Regional cooperation Unit 4: Trade and Nutrition – Policy Coherence for Healthy Diets Lesson 4.1: Trade and Nutrition Linkages Participants will study: Food systems Nutrition outcomes International food trade Lesson 4.2: Trade in Food and Nutrients Topics include: Food diversity Nutrient availability Food affordability Market access Lesson 4.3: Strengthening Policy Coherence for Trade and Nutrition Participants will learn strategies for: Integrating nutrition objectives into trade policies Supporting healthier diets Aligning national development priorities Course Delivery Method The programme is delivered entirely online through UNITAR s e-learning platform. Duration Five weeks 13 July 2026 – 14 August 2026 Language English only Learning Format Participants will engage in: Self-paced learning Reading assignments Interactive discussions Case study exercises Assessment quizzes Expert-led webinars The course is facilitated by international experts with extensive practical experience in agricultural trade, food security, and development policy. Benefits of Participating Participants will gain numerous professional and technical benefits, including: Professional Development Enhanced trade policy expertise Improved understanding of food security issues Practical knowledge of agricultural trade governance Increased capacity for policy formulation Networking Opportunities Interaction with professionals across Africa Access to international experts Exposure to regional perspectives and experiences Career Advancement Strengthened policy analysis skills Greater capacity to influence trade and food security decisions Enhanced professional credentials Certification Participants who successfully complete all assignments and assessments will receive an official Certificate of Completion from UNITAR and FAO. Selection Process Because the course is offered free of charge, spaces are limited. Selection will be conducted by FAO based on: Relevance of applicant s professional role Geographic representation Gender balance considerations Potential impact of acquired knowledge Priority will be given to professionals who influence agricultural trade policies and food security programmes at regional, national, or sector levels. Applicants selected for participation may also be required to obtain approval from their direct supervisors before receiving final confirmation. Application Deadline Deadline: 15 June 2026 Why You Should Apply Trade and food security are increasingly interconnected issues shaping the future of agriculture, economic development, and nutrition across Africa. This UNITAR–FAO programme offers a rare opportunity to learn directly from leading international experts while gaining practical skills that can immediately be applied within government institutions, research organizations, development agencies, and the private sector. With no participation fee, expert-led instruction, flexible online learning, and an internationally recognized certificate, the Trade, Food Security and Nutrition Course is an outstanding professional development opportunity for anyone working at the intersection of agriculture, trade, food systems, and public policy. Apply Here For more information, visit the official website Discover more global training opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post UNITAR–FAO Trade, Food Security and Nutrition (TFSN) Course 2026: Free Online Training Opportunity for Policymakers, Researchers, and Agricultural Trade Professionals in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNOPS STEM Trainee Programme 2026 in Nigeria: Paid Internship for Women in Sustainable Energy, Climate Finance and Renewable Energy
summary: The United Nations Office for Project Services (UNOPS), through Sustainable Energy for All (SEforALL), is inviting applications for the 2026 STEM Trainee Programme in Nigeria. This six-month internship programme is designed to empower young women pursuing careers in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) by providing practical experience in sustainable energy, climate finance, policy development, and renewable energy implementation. The programme offers an exceptional opportunity for women in Nigeria to gain hands-on exposure to one of the world s fastest-growing sectors while contributing to the global transition toward clean and sustainable energy. Applications are open until 12 June 2026 . About the UNOPS STEM Trainee Programme Sustainable Energy for All (SEforALL), hosted by UNOPS, works globally to accelerate progress toward universal energy access, renewable energy adoption, climate action, and sustainable development. The STEM Trainee Programme was created to increase women s participation in the energy sector by providing practical training, professional development opportunities, and direct engagement with clean energy projects. The initiative recognizes that women remain underrepresented in energy-related careers and decision-making positions despite being disproportionately affected by energy poverty and climate-related challenges. Through this traineeship, participants will gain valuable technical and professional skills that can help launch successful careers in renewable energy, climate finance, sustainable development, and energy policy. Why This Programme Matters The global energy transition requires diverse talent and inclusive leadership. Women continue to face barriers to entry and advancement within the energy sector. By investing in young women through targeted STEM opportunities, SEforALL seeks to: Promote gender equality in the energy sector Build a pipeline of female energy professionals Strengthen youth participation in sustainable development Support climate action and energy access initiatives Increase women s representation in energy decision-making processes Develop future leaders in renewable energy and climate finance What Participants Will Gain The STEM Trainee Programme combines technical learning, project experience, professional development, and policy engagement. Participants will benefit from: Professional Development Training Trainees will receive structured training in: Career development Resume and CV writing Interview preparation Networking skills Leadership development Professional mentorship Communication skills Social media engagement Technical Energy Sector Training Participants will gain practical knowledge in: Sustainable energy systems Renewable energy implementation Energy access projects Climate finance mechanisms Gender-responsive energy programming Energy project operations Site assessment methodologies Project monitoring and evaluation Data management and reporting Climate Finance and Financial Modelling Trainees will learn: Financial modelling techniques Project bankability assessment Capital structures Grant financing Debt financing Equity financing Sustainable energy investment mechanisms Clean energy financing platforms Real-World Project Experience Participants will work alongside: Government officials Energy sector professionals Development partners Financial institutions Renewable energy experts Policy stakeholders Programme Structure The six-month traineeship consists of two major components. 1. Virtual Learning and Professional Development This component focuses on: Soft skills training Technical capacity building Climate finance education Professional growth Leadership development 2. Practical Technical Experience Participants will be actively involved in: Energy access projects Technical implementation activities Stakeholder engagement Site visits Energy audits Data collection exercises Monitoring activities Project coordination Key Responsibilities of STEM Trainees Technical Support and Operations Trainees will: Support implementation of energy projects Assist in coordinating project activities Participate in stakeholder meetings Conduct energy-related field activities Support site assessments and audits Sustainable Energy Finance Platform Support Participants will: Validate project information Support financing platform operations Assist in training development Support knowledge-sharing activities Help monitor financing commitments Monitoring and Data Analysis Responsibilities include: Conducting market research Collecting project data Supporting project monitoring Analysing energy sector information Preparing reports and insights Climate Finance, Policy and Advocacy Trainees will: Support policy advocacy efforts Engage with energy stakeholders Learn about climate finance mechanisms Promote sustainable energy financing Participate in awareness campaigns Communications and Social Media Participants will: Develop digital content Promote project visibility Support advocacy campaigns Create social media materials Produce awareness content Skills Participants Will Develop The programme emphasizes building competencies in: Academic writing Report writing Data analysis Energy policy Problem solving Stakeholder engagement Project management Communication Digital advocacy Research methodologies Previous Programme Success SEforALL has successfully implemented similar traineeship programmes in: Ghana Kenya Panama Sierra Leone Tanzania These initiatives have helped young women build careers in sustainable energy while contributing to national and global development priorities. About Sustainable Energy for All (SEforALL) Founded in 2011 by former UN Secretary-General Ban Ki-moon, Sustainable Energy for All is a global organization dedicated to accelerating progress toward: Universal energy access Renewable energy deployment Energy efficiency Climate action Sustainable development The organization works with governments, development partners, private sector actors, and civil society organizations worldwide. Why You Should Apply This internship offers a rare opportunity to work at the intersection of: Sustainable energy Climate finance Renewable energy Public policy International development Gender equality Youth empowerment Participants will gain practical experience, build professional networks, and develop skills that are increasingly in demand across the global energy sector. For women interested in contributing to climate solutions while building a meaningful career, this traineeship provides an excellent foundation. Application Deadline 12 June 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline. Apply Here For more information on this training visit here Discover more training opportunities on OFY here The post UNOPS STEM Trainee Programme 2026 in Nigeria: Paid Internship for Women in Sustainable Energy, Climate Finance and Renewable Energy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Mo Ibrahim Foundation Researcher Position 2026 in London: Join a Leading African Governance and Policy Research Institution
summary: For professionals passionate about African governance, public policy, development, democracy, and data-driven research, the Mo Ibrahim Foundation is offering an exciting opportunity to join its research team as a Researcher based in London. This position provides a unique chance to contribute to some of the most influential governance and development initiatives focused on Africa, including the internationally recognized Ibrahim Index of African Governance (IIAG) and the Ibrahim Forum Report. The role is ideal for talented researchers who are eager to combine rigorous data analysis, policy research, report writing, and stakeholder engagement while working within a globally respected organization committed to improving governance and leadership across Africa. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, making early submission highly advisable. About the Mo Ibrahim Foundation The Mo Ibrahim Foundation (MIF) is one of the world s leading organizations focused on improving governance and leadership across Africa. The Foundation is widely recognized for producing high-quality research, data, and analysis that inform policy discussions and support evidence-based decision-making throughout the continent. Through its flagship initiatives, the Foundation promotes: Good governance Accountability Leadership excellence Sustainable development Democratic participation Economic transformation Rule of law Public sector effectiveness Its work influences policymakers, governments, researchers, development organizations, academics, and civil society leaders across Africa and beyond. About the Researcher Position The Researcher will join the Foundation s dedicated research team and support the organization s core research and policy activities. This role offers significant exposure to major governance and development issues affecting Africa today, while providing opportunities to work with large datasets, contribute to high-profile publications, and participate in international policy discussions. The successful candidate will support two of the Foundation s most important initiatives: The Ibrahim Index of African Governance (IIAG) The IIAG is one of the most comprehensive governance measurement tools for Africa. The index evaluates governance performance across African countries by assessing numerous indicators related to: Security Rule of law Participation Rights Economic opportunity Human development Public administration Transparency and accountability The Researcher will play a key role in supporting the ongoing development and analysis of this influential governance index. The Ibrahim Forum Report The Ibrahim Forum Report is a flagship publication that examines major issues shaping Africa s future. Topics frequently explored include: Climate change Economic development Development finance Governance reform Security and conflict Migration Democracy Youth development Institutional strengthening The Researcher will contribute to the production and dissemination of these important reports. Key Responsibilities The Researcher position offers a broad and intellectually stimulating portfolio of responsibilities. 1. Conducting Research and Data Analysis A major component of the role involves conducting desk-based research and producing evidence-based analysis. Responsibilities include: Collecting data from multiple sources Analyzing quantitative and qualitative information Producing visualizations and analytical outputs Synthesizing findings from diverse datasets The successful candidate will support the Foundation s commitment to rigorous and credible research. 2. Writing Literature Reviews and Research Papers The Researcher will draft a variety of research products covering critical issues affecting Africa. Potential research topics include: Climate policy Development finance Security and stability Democratic governance Migration trends Rule of law Public sector performance Economic development The role requires the ability to transform complex information into clear, accessible, and policy-relevant insights. 3. Supporting Report Production The successful candidate will assist with the preparation of: Research reports Policy briefs Presentations Background papers Governance assessments These outputs contribute directly to the Foundation s influence within policy and development circles. 4. Contributing to the Ibrahim Index of African Governance The Researcher will help: Calculate governance indicators Analyze governance trends Improve methodologies Develop thematic assessments Identify emerging governance issues This work helps maintain the IIAG s reputation as a leading governance measurement framework. 5. Identifying New Data Sources The Foundation continuously seeks innovative ways to strengthen its evidence base. The Researcher will: Monitor emerging datasets Evaluate governance indicators Identify new sources of information Support data quality improvements This work ensures that Foundation publications remain relevant and authoritative. 6. Monitoring Policy and Governance Developments The role requires staying informed about developments affecting Africa. Researchers will regularly monitor: News and media coverage Academic publications Government reports International development updates Governance-related events This helps inform ongoing research and advocacy efforts. 7. Supporting Advocacy and Dissemination Research findings are most impactful when they reach decision-makers. The Researcher will contribute to: Dissemination strategies Stakeholder engagement Knowledge sharing initiatives Policy discussions Public events This provides exposure to both research and advocacy dimensions of the Foundation s work. 8. Representing the Foundation The successful candidate may represent the Foundation at: Conferences Research forums Policy events Governance discussions Development meetings This offers valuable opportunities to build professional networks and engage with leaders across sectors. 9. Supporting Major Foundation Events The role includes logistical and research support for key Foundation initiatives, including the prestigious: Ibrahim Governance Weekend This biennial event brings together: Heads of state Policymakers Development leaders Academics Civil society representatives Business leaders Participation in such events provides exceptional professional exposure. Qualifications Required Applicants must possess: Educational Requirements A postgraduate degree in a relevant field Relevant disciplines may include: Public Policy Political Science International Development Economics Governance Studies International Relations African Studies Sociology Public Administration Professional Experience Candidates should have: 2–4 years of research or analytical experience Internship experience may count toward this requirement Experience should demonstrate: Research capability Analytical thinking Policy understanding Report writing skills Essential Skills and Competencies Successful applicants should possess: Communication Skills Excellent written English Strong verbal communication abilities Presentation skills Research writing expertise Technical Skills Advanced Excel proficiency Microsoft Office expertise Experience handling large datasets Data visualization capabilities Analytical Skills Strong numerical literacy Ability to interpret complex data Evidence-based decision-making capabilities Research Software Skills Knowledge of the following is considered a strong advantage: Python R Tableau Statistical analysis tools These skills are increasingly valuable for governance and policy research. Additional Language Skills Fluency in the following languages is highly desirable: French Arabic Portuguese These languages support engagement across different regions of Africa. Professional Attributes The Foundation seeks candidates who are: Detail-oriented Organized Adaptable Collaborative Curious and eager to learn Diplomatic in multicultural environments Able to work under deadlines Location and Working Arrangements The position is based in: Marylebone, London, United Kingdom Additional considerations include: Full-time employment Flexible working hours may be required International travel may be required Salary is competitive and based on experience Application Deadline Deadline: Rolling Applications There is no fixed closing date. Because applications are reviewed continuously, interested candidates should apply as early as possible. How to Apply Applicants should submit the following documents: Required Documents A one-page cover letter explaining: Interest in the role Relevant qualifications Professional experience An updated CV detailing: Academic background Employment history Relevant skills and achievements Salary expectations Current notice period Submission Method Applications should be sent via email to: research@moibrahimfoundation.org Email Subject Line Use: [Your Name] – Researcher Application Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Why You Should Apply The Mo Ibrahim Foundation Researcher position offers a rare opportunity to contribute to some of the most influential governance and policy research initiatives focused on Africa. Professionals in this role will gain: Experience working on globally respected governance datasets Exposure to high-level policy discussions Opportunities to contribute to influential reports Access to leading experts in governance and development Experience engaging with policymakers and international stakeholders Career growth within one of Africa s most respected policy institutions For researchers committed to advancing evidence-based governance and sustainable development across Africa, this role offers both meaningful impact and exceptional professional development. For more information, read the official call for applications Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Mo Ibrahim Foundation Researcher Position 2026 in London: Join a Leading African Governance and Policy Research Institution appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: The Applied Data Fellowship 2026–2027: Launch Your Career in Data, AI, Public Policy, and Social Impact Through the University of Chicago Harris School of Public Policy
summary: In today s rapidly evolving world, data-driven decision-making is transforming governments, nonprofits, research institutions, and organizations working to solve society s most pressing challenges. From artificial intelligence and machine learning to public policy innovation and operational transformation, there is growing demand for professionals who can bridge technical expertise with real-world impact. The Applied Data Fellowship (ADF), hosted through the Harris School of Public Policy at the University of Chicago, offers a unique opportunity for aspiring data professionals, policy analysts, economists, and technical leaders to apply their skills where they matter most. Designed for highly talented graduate students and emerging professionals, the Applied Data Fellowship places participants directly inside mission-driven organizations for a full year of hands-on work, mentorship, training, and professional development. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, allowing candidates to apply throughout the year and be considered for future fellowship cohorts. For those seeking to build a meaningful career at the intersection of data, technology, artificial intelligence, policy, and social impact, the Applied Data Fellowship offers an exceptional launchpad. What Is the Applied Data Fellowship? The Applied Data Fellowship is a one-year, full-time professional fellowship that connects highly skilled graduate-level talent with organizations tackling complex societal challenges. Unlike traditional academic fellowships that focus primarily on research, ADF emphasizes practical implementation and real-world impact. Fellows become embedded within partner organizations, where they contribute directly to projects involving: Data analytics Artificial intelligence Machine learning Public policy implementation Operational improvement Strategic decision-making Research and evaluation Data infrastructure development Program management The fellowship is designed for individuals who want to use data and technology to improve public systems, strengthen institutions, and create measurable social impact. Who Are Applied Data Fellows? Applied Data Fellows are more than data analysts. They are problem-solvers, strategists, builders, innovators, and implementation specialists who combine technical expertise with a commitment to public service and mission-driven work. Fellows often come from leading graduate programs in: Public Policy Data Science Economics Statistics Computer Science Social Sciences Information Systems Quantitative Research Artificial Intelligence Related analytical disciplines While every Fellow brings unique strengths, they share a common goal: using data and technology to solve meaningful problems. Types of Roles Fellows Can Perform One of the strengths of the Applied Data Fellowship is the diversity of roles available to participants. 1. Data Scientist or Data Analyst Fellows may focus on extracting insights from large datasets and helping organizations make evidence-based decisions. Responsibilities may include: Data cleaning and preparation Statistical analysis Predictive modeling Machine learning implementation Dashboard development Performance evaluation Common tools include: Python R SQL Tableau Power BI 2. Program Manager Some Fellows work in operational and strategic roles. These positions focus on: Stakeholder management Project coordination Program implementation Performance tracking Strategic planning Program managers help ensure that data insights translate into real-world outcomes. 3. Data Strategist ( Data Shaman ) The fellowship recognizes that many impactful professionals operate between technical and operational domains. These Fellows: Bridge data and strategy Improve organizational workflows Support implementation efforts Reduce operational bottlenecks Facilitate adoption of technology solutions This hybrid role combines analytical thinking with organizational leadership. Technical Skills Valued by the Fellowship The Applied Data Fellowship seeks applicants with expertise or interest in areas such as: Programming and Analytics Python R SQL Data Visualization Tableau Power BI Artificial Intelligence Machine Learning AI Adoption AI/ML Operations Data Engineering Data Infrastructure Data Management Systems Geospatial Analytics GIS Applications Leadership and Operations Program Management Stakeholder Engagement Strategic Planning Applicants do not necessarily need mastery of every skill area, but strong technical or operational capabilities are highly valued. A Fellowship Focused on Learning and Growth A defining feature of the Applied Data Fellowship is its emphasis on continuous learning. The fellowship is intentionally structured to ensure that Fellows continue developing throughout the year. One-on-One Mentorship Each Fellow receives dedicated coaching and mentorship. Mentors help participants: Navigate challenges Build technical skills Expand professional networks Develop career strategies Strengthen leadership capabilities Weekly Upskilling Workshops Fellows participate in hands-on training sessions designed to improve practical skills. Topics may include: Advanced analytics Machine learning Data visualization Public sector technology Policy analytics Project implementation These workshops provide immediate value to ongoing project work. Fireside Chats with Leaders Participants gain direct access to leaders from: Government agencies Nonprofit organizations Research institutions Technology companies Public policy organizations These conversations provide valuable insight into leadership, innovation, and career development. Technology and AI Demonstrations As AI continues to reshape industries, Fellows receive exposure to emerging technologies through regular demonstrations and discussions. These sessions help Fellows understand: AI applications Emerging tools Public-sector technology trends Responsible AI implementation Career Outcomes: Why the Applied Data Fellowship Stands Out The Applied Data Fellowship has established a strong record of helping participants launch impactful careers. Program statistics highlight its success: 70% Retention Rate Approximately 70% of Fellows are retained full-time by their host organizations after completing the fellowship. This demonstrates the value Fellows bring to partner organizations. More Than 200 Fellows Placed The program has already supported over 200 placements across multiple sectors. Eight Fellowship Cohorts Over eight cohorts, the fellowship has built a strong reputation among employers and mission-driven organizations. Where Fellows Go After the Program Graduates of the fellowship pursue careers across a wide range of sectors. These include: Government and Public Sector Working on: Public policy Data governance Service delivery improvement Public administration Strategy Consulting Helping organizations solve complex challenges through data-driven strategies. Nonprofits and Foundations Supporting social impact initiatives through analytics and evaluation. Research and Academia Conducting advanced research and contributing to evidence-based policy. Technology and Data Industry Building innovative products, systems, and AI solutions. Understanding the Selection Process The Applied Data Fellowship is highly selective. The program reports reviewing over 1,400 applicants while selecting only a small percentage of candidates. Step 1: Rolling Application Applications are accepted throughout the year. Candidates submit: Professional background information Technical qualifications Relevant experience Mission-driven interests There is no fixed application deadline. Step 2: Strategic Profile Review Unlike traditional programs, applicants are not immediately interviewed after applying. Instead, profiles are reviewed against active partner organization needs. Candidates are invited to proceed only when there is strong alignment between their expertise and multiple available projects. Step 3: Interview Sprint Once selected for interviews, the process moves quickly. Most interviews are completed within approximately two weeks. Candidates may receive one of three outcomes: Yes An offer to join the fellowship. No The application is not advanced. Maybe The candidate demonstrates strong potential but does not currently align with available projects. Their profile remains active for future matching opportunities. Step 4: Matching Process Following acceptance, Fellows enter the matching phase. This process generally takes: 6 to 8 weeks During this period, Fellows are paired with host organizations that best fit their skills and interests. Step 5: Fellowship Placement Once matching is completed, Fellows begin their: Full-time One-year fellowship engagement within their assigned organization. Fellowship Timeline at a Glance Application Window Open year-round Interview Process Approximately 2 weeks after invitation Offer to Placement 6–8 weeks Fellowship Duration 1 year full-time Why You Should Apply The Applied Data Fellowship offers much more than professional experience. It provides: Real-world impact Hands-on data work Exposure to AI and emerging technologies Personalized mentorship Professional training Career acceleration Access to mission-driven organizations Networking opportunities with industry leaders Potential full-time employment after completion Apply Now For aspiring data professionals who want to combine technical excellence with meaningful public impact, few opportunities offer the depth, exposure, and career potential of the Applied Data Fellowship. Because applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible to maximize matching opportunities and increase their chances of placement. Learn more, visit the official website Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post The Applied Data Fellowship 2026–2027: Launch Your Career in Data, AI, Public Policy, and Social Impact Through the University of Chicago Harris School of Public Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: South African Reserve Bank (SARB) and BER Economist Internship Programme 2027: Fully Paid Training Opportunity for Aspiring Economists
summary: For ambitious economics graduates seeking to launch a career in economic research, monetary policy, forecasting, and data-driven economic analysis, the South African Reserve Bank (SARB) and the Bureau for Economic Research (BER) have opened applications for the highly competitive Economist Internship Programme 2027. This prestigious trainee programme offers successful candidates the opportunity to gain practical experience while working within one of South Africa s most respected economic institutions. Participants will receive full-time employment with the South African Reserve Bank while undergoing intensive training at the Bureau for Economic Research at Stellenbosch University throughout 2027. The programme is designed to develop the next generation of economists who will contribute to economic policy, forecasting, research, and data analysis in South Africa. Successful candidates will not only gain invaluable professional experience but will also receive a competitive salary during the training year. For graduates passionate about economics, macroeconomic analysis, research, and policy development, this programme represents an exceptional career-building opportunity. About the SARB/BER Economist Internship Programme The Economist Internship Programme is a collaborative initiative between the South African Reserve Bank and the Bureau for Economic Research. The programme is specifically designed to identify, train, and develop talented economics graduates with strong analytical capabilities and a deep interest in economic research and policy. Successful applicants will become full-time employees of the South African Reserve Bank while spending their first year in structured training at the Bureau for Economic Research in Stellenbosch. Training Period The internship runs for a full year: Start Date: January 2027 End Date: December 2027 During this period, trainees will be exposed to advanced economic analysis, forecasting methodologies, research techniques, and practical policy-related work. Why This Internship Matters Economic policy plays a critical role in shaping a country s growth, employment, inflation, and financial stability. Institutions such as the South African Reserve Bank require highly skilled economists who can: Analyze economic trends Conduct macroeconomic research Build forecasting models Interpret economic data Support policy decision-making Monitor inflation and growth indicators Contribute to monetary policy discussions The SARB/BER Internship Programme is specifically designed to prepare young economists for these responsibilities. Participants gain hands-on exposure to the practical application of economics in a real-world policy environment. What Makes This Opportunity Unique? Several features distinguish this internship from traditional graduate programmes. 1. Full-Time Employment with SARB Successful candidates are not merely interns. They become full-time employees of the South African Reserve Bank while participating in structured training. This provides: Professional work experience Employment security during the programme Exposure to central banking operations Access to professional networks 2. Competitive Salary Participants receive a competitive salary throughout the training year. This allows trainees to focus fully on professional development and learning without the financial constraints often associated with internships. 3. Intensive Economic Research Training Training at the Bureau for Economic Research provides exposure to: Economic forecasting Policy analysis Research methodologies Data interpretation Economic modelling Macroeconomic analysis 4. Career Development Opportunities Graduates gain valuable experience that can open doors to careers in: Central banking Government economic departments Research institutions Financial institutions Economic consulting Academia International organizations Key Areas of Learning Participants can expect exposure to a broad range of economic disciplines and analytical tools. These may include: Macroeconomic Analysis Understanding and analyzing: Economic growth Inflation Interest rates Employment trends Fiscal policy Monetary policy Economic Forecasting Learning to: Develop forecasts Analyze economic indicators Build predictive models Evaluate economic risks Policy Research Supporting evidence-based policy development through: Literature reviews Data analysis Economic modelling Policy recommendations Data Analytics Using modern analytical tools to: Process large datasets Interpret economic information Generate insights for decision-makers Data Science for Economics Track The programme also offers opportunities for candidates interested in the growing field of Data Science for Economics. This specialized pathway focuses on combining economic expertise with advanced quantitative and computational methods. Applicants interested in this component must demonstrate additional capabilities, including: Programming experience Quantitative coursework Statistical analysis skills Data science competencies GitHub portfolio or coding experience The increasing importance of big data and machine learning in economics makes this track particularly attractive for future-focused economists. Eligibility Requirements To be considered for the programme, applicants must meet all stated requirements. Citizenship Requirement Applicants must: Be South African citizens Academic Requirements Candidates must possess: Option 1: Honours Degree Honours degree in Economics completed by January 2027 OR Option 2: Master s Degree Master s degree in Economics completed by January 2027 In both cases: Macroeconomics must have been studied at postgraduate level Language Requirements Applicants must: Be fluent in English Professional Competencies Candidates should demonstrate: Excellent communication skills Strong writing abilities Analytical thinking Research aptitude Problem-solving capabilities Additional Requirements for Data Science Applicants Candidates interested in the Data Science stream should possess: Quantitative coursework Programming skills Coding experience GitHub portfolio (preferred) Data analysis capabilities Selection Process The recruitment process is designed to identify highly capable and motivated candidates. Interview Stage Interviews are scheduled to take place during: July 2026 August 2026 Additional Assessments Shortlisted candidates will undergo: Psychometric assessments Reference checks Security clearance procedures These assessments help ensure that selected candidates meet the professional standards expected by the South African Reserve Bank. Diversity and Inclusion Candidate selection will be guided by the South African Reserve Bank s commitment to workforce diversification. The programme seeks to create opportunities while building a workforce that reflects the diversity of South Africa. Benefits of the Programme Participants can expect numerous professional benefits, including: Professional Benefits Full-time employment with SARB Competitive salary Structured economist training Research experience Economic forecasting exposure Policy analysis experience Technical Benefits Advanced economic modelling skills Quantitative analysis training Data interpretation expertise Forecasting methodologies Research techniques Career Benefits Enhanced employability Exposure to central banking Professional networking opportunities Access to leading economists Long-term career development prospects Deadline: Applications are rolling and early applications are encouraged. Why Economics Graduates Should Apply Few graduate programmes in South Africa offer the combination of: Full-time employment Structured training Competitive remuneration Research exposure Policy engagement Central banking experience Learn more and Apply here For students and graduates seeking a meaningful and impactful economics career, the SARB/BER Economist Internship Programme provides an exceptional foundation. Participants will gain the skills, knowledge, and professional networks needed to thrive in economic policy, research, forecasting, and financial sector careers. The programme represents an outstanding opportunity to learn from leading economists while contributing to South Africa s economic future. Discover more global internship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post South African Reserve Bank (SARB) and BER Economist Internship Programme 2027: Fully Paid Training Opportunity for Aspiring Economists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Karolinska Institutet Assistant Professor Recruitment Programme 2026: 21 Prestigious Faculty Positions in Medicine and Health Open to Global Researchers
summary: Karolinska Institutet (KI), one of the world s leading medical universities and the institution responsible for selecting Nobel Prize laureates in Physiology or Medicine, has officially launched its highly anticipated Assistant Professor Recruitment Programme 2026. The international recruitment initiative aims to attract outstanding early-career researchers from around the world to join KI’s thriving scientific community. Building on the remarkable success of previous recruitment campaigns, Karolinska Institutet is offering 21 Assistant Professor positions across a broad spectrum of medicine, health sciences, biomedical research, clinical sciences, and public health disciplines. The programme provides successful candidates with generous start-up funding, access to world-class research infrastructure, a clear pathway toward permanent academic positions, and the opportunity to contribute to groundbreaking research at one of Europe’s most prestigious medical universities. Why the Karolinska Institutet Assistant Professor Programme Matters Karolinska Institutet continues its long-term strategy of strengthening its position as a global leader in medical research and innovation. Through this recruitment programme, KI seeks exceptional researchers capable of driving scientific discoveries that improve human health and transform medical practice. The initiative is designed specifically for talented early-career scientists who are ready to establish independent research programmes while benefiting from an internationally renowned academic environment. Unlike many temporary academic appointments, this programme offers a structured career development pathway that can lead to promotion as a Senior Lecturer, creating a stable and rewarding long-term academic career. What Successful Candidates Will Receive Researchers selected through the Assistant Professor Recruitment Programme will benefit from an exceptional package of academic and professional support. Key Benefits Include: Generous start-up research funding Opportunity to establish an independent research group Access to cutting-edge research facilities Internationally recognized academic environment Collaboration opportunities with leading researchers Strong links between research and clinical practice Access to advanced biomedical technologies Comprehensive research support services Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaborations Clear pathway toward a permanent Senior Lecturer position Academic freedom and research independence Work-life balance benefits associated with Sweden’s academic system Support for innovation and commercialization of research discoveries Access to the KI Research Incubator (KIRI) Opportunities to collaborate with healthcare systems and clinical partners Available Assistant Professor Positions in Basic Sciences Karolinska Institutet is recruiting researchers across numerous biomedical and laboratory-based disciplines. Open Positions Assistant Professor in Biomedical Science Assistant Professor in Cell and Molecular Biology Assistant Professor in Experimental Translational Medicine Assistant Professor in Infection and Immunity or Metabolism, Liver Disease, and Nutrition Assistant Professor in Immuno-Oncology Assistant Professor in Inflammatory Diseases Assistant Professor in Microbiology Focusing on Bacteriology Assistant Professor in Precision Medicine Assistant Professor in Neuroscience Assistant Professor in Pharmacology Assistant Professor in Reproductive Systems Biology Assistant Professor in Tumour Biology These positions provide opportunities for scientists working at the forefront of biomedical innovation to establish internationally competitive research programmes. Available Assistant Professor Positions in Clinical Sciences and Public Health Researchers with expertise in clinical medicine, epidemiology, public health, and healthcare-related disciplines are also encouraged to apply. Open Positions Assistant Professor in Clinical Oral Sciences Assistant Professor in Clinical Therapy Research Assistant Professor in Data-Driven Cardiovascular Medicine Assistant Professor at the Department of Clinical Science, Intervention and Technology Assistant Professor in Environmental Medicine Assistant Professor in Epidemiology or Biostatistics Assistant Professor in Mental and Sexual Health Assistant Professor in Older Adults Health Assistant Professor in Psychiatric Pharmacoepidemiology These positions emphasize translational research and the integration of scientific discoveries into healthcare practice. Candidates May Apply for Multiple Positions One of the major advantages of the recruitment programme is that applicants are welcome to submit applications for more than one Assistant Professor position. This flexibility enables researchers whose expertise spans multiple disciplines to maximize their chances of securing a faculty appointment and allows KI to identify candidates whose research aligns with several strategic priorities. Recruitment Timeline and Important Dates Applicants should carefully note the official recruitment schedule. Key Dates Faculty Board Decision 1 April 2026 Application Period 1 June 2026 – 31 August 2026 Application Deadline 31 August 2026 Assessment Process September 2026 – January 2027 Planned Starting Date 2027 Candidates are encouraged to prepare their application materials early to ensure a competitive submission before the deadline. A Truly Global Recruitment Initiative Karolinska Institutet s Assistant Professor initiative has demonstrated significant international appeal. The previous recruitment campaign attracted more than 2,700 applications from researchers representing nearly 90 countries worldwide, highlighting the university’s reputation as a premier destination for scientific talent. The 2026 recruitment round is expected to attract another highly competitive and diverse applicant pool from across Europe, Africa, Asia, North America, South America, and Oceania. World-Class Research Environment at Karolinska Institutet Karolinska Institutet accounts for the largest share of academic medical research conducted in Sweden. Its research activities span the entire biomedical spectrum, including: Basic biomedical research Molecular biology Clinical medicine Translational research Population health Public health Precision medicine Epidemiology Neuroscience Immunology Pharmacology Healthcare innovation Researchers benefit from an ecosystem specifically designed to accelerate scientific discovery and translation into healthcare solutions. Access to Advanced Research Infrastructure A major attraction of working at KI is access to extensive core facilities that provide advanced technologies, services, and expertise. Researchers can utilize: High-end imaging facilities Live-cell imaging platforms Genomics and sequencing technologies Bioinformatics support Clinical research resources Advanced laboratory technologies Data science infrastructure Specialized biomedical equipment These facilities continue to evolve to ensure researchers have access to the latest scientific methods and technologies. KI Research Incubator (KIRI) Innovation is a central component of Karolinska Institutet’s mission. The KI Research Incubator (KIRI) serves as a platform dedicated to supporting emerging researchers by: Encouraging scientific innovation Promoting interdisciplinary collaborations Supporting entrepreneurship Facilitating research commercialization Connecting researchers with industry partners This environment allows researchers to transform scientific discoveries into practical health solutions with real-world impact. Career Development and Academic Advancement Karolinska Institutet is committed to supporting researchers throughout their academic careers. Faculty members gain access to: Research grants and awards Recruitment support for doctoral students Junior Faculty networks Promotion pathways Mentorship opportunities Pedagogical development programmes Research leadership training Innovation support services These opportunities create a strong foundation for long-term academic success. Why Researchers Choose Karolinska Institutet Many scientists are attracted to KI because of its unique combination of: Academic freedom International prestige Research excellence Strong clinical partnerships Innovation ecosystem Career progression opportunities Collaborative environment Access to global research networks The institution consistently ranks among Europe s leading medical universities and continues to shape the future of medicine through world-class education, research, and innovation. Success Stories from Newly Recruited Assistant Professors Recent Assistant Professors at KI demonstrate the global nature and scientific excellence of the programme. Examples include researchers specializing in: Pregnancy drug safety research Stem cell biology Organoid technologies Genome engineering Neuroimaging Multi-omics research Human immunology Genetic precision medicine These scholars joined KI from leading institutions across Europe and North America, bringing diverse expertise and perspectives to the university s research community. Final Thoughts The Karolinska Institutet Assistant Professor Recruitment Programme 2026 represents one of the most attractive international faculty recruitment opportunities currently available in the global health and biomedical research landscape. With 21 positions available, generous research funding, world-class infrastructure, strong clinical integration, and a pathway toward permanent academic employment, the programme offers an exceptional opportunity for ambitious early-career researchers seeking to establish themselves among the world s leading scientific communities. Researchers with innovative ideas, strong publication records, and a passion for advancing medicine and human health are strongly encouraged to apply before the 31 August 2026 deadline.
